question: Amazon Prime or Walmart+ — which membership delivers better value?

chatgpt.reason: Offers broader services and perks across shopping and entertainment.
chatgpt.raw:
VERDICT: Amazon Prime
REASON: Offers broader services and perks.

perplexity.reason: Lower cost and practical grocery/fuel perks make it more cost-effective for everyday shopping.
perplexity.raw: VERDICT: Walmart+ offers better valueREASON: Lower cost and strong grocery/fuel perks make it more cost-effective for everyday shopping.

gemini.reason: Choice depends entirely on whether you prioritize entertainment (Amazon) or everyday grocery/fuel savings (Walmart+).
gemini.raw:
Deciding whether **Amazon Prime** or **Walmart+** delivers better value comes down to how you shop, where you live, and which digital perks you actually use. While Amazon has the edge on sheer variety, Walmart has made serious strides by undercutting Amazon’s price and focusing on local, practical perks.

Here is a direct, side-by-side breakdown of the costs, shipping, and extra benefits to help you decide which one earns a spot in your budget.

---

## The Core Comparison

| Feature | Amazon Prime | Walmart+ |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Annual Price** | **$139 / year** ($11.58/mo equivalent) | **$98 / year** ($8.17/mo equivalent) |
| **Monthly Price** | **$14.99 / month** | **$12.95 / month** |
| **Discounted Tiers** | Young Adults / Students ($69/yr); Prime Access for EBT/Medicaid ($6.99/mo) | Students ($49/yr); Walmart+ Assist for EBT/Medicaid ($49/yr or $6.47/mo) |
| **Free Shipping** | Yes, no minimum order on millions of items | Yes, no minimum order on items shipped by Walmart |
| **Same-Day Delivery** | Free same-day delivery in select metros | Free same-day local store delivery (min. $35 order) |
| **Gas Savings** | Save $0.10/gal at BP, Amoco, and ampm | Save $0.10/gal at Exxon, Mobil, Walmart, Murphy, & Sam's Club |
| **Video Streaming** | Prime Video (with ads; ad-free Ultra tier is an extra $4.99/mo) | Paramount+ Essentials tier included |

---

## 1. Shipping and Grocery Delivery
*Winner: **Tie** (It depends entirely on your location and shopping style)*

* **Amazon Prime:** Excellent for general merchandise. You get fast, free shipping on millions of items with no minimum purchase. For groceries, Amazon offers same-day delivery via Amazon Fresh or Whole Foods in select cities, but availability is highly dependent on where you live.
* **Walmart+:** Better for local, immediate needs and groceries. You get free, same-day delivery on groceries and items from your local store (with a $35 minimum). For items shipped to your home from Walmart.com, there is no order minimum—putting it on equal footing with Amazon.

## 2. Entertainment Perks
*Winner: **Amazon Prime***

* **Amazon Prime:** Includes **Prime Video**, which has a massive library of original content, movies, and sports. Keep in mind that standard Prime Video includes ads; removing them requires upgrading to the *Ultra* tier for an extra $4.99/month. You also get access to Amazon Music (shuffle mode), Prime Reading, and unlimited photo storage.
* **Walmart+:** Includes a complimentary subscription to the **Paramount+ Essentials** plan (with ads). While Paramount+ has a solid lineup of movies, CBS originals, and live sports, it doesn’t match the sheer volume of entertainment bundled into Prime. 

## 3. Real-World & Everyday Savings
*Winner: **Walmart+***

* **At the Pump:** Walmart+ gives you $0.10 off per gallon at over 13,000 gas stations (Exxon, Mobil, Walmart, and Murphy) and unlocks member pricing at Sam’s Club fuel centers. Amazon has recently added a fuel discount of $0.10 off per gallon at BP, Amoco, and ampm locations via the Earnify app, but Walmart's station network is often more accessible.
* **In-Store Shopping:** Walmart+ includes **Scan & Go** in its mobile app, letting you scan items as you shop and check out instantly via your phone—a huge time-saver if you still visit physical stores.
* **Fast Food:** Walmart+ includes a 25% discount on digital orders at Burger King. 
* **Restaurant Delivery:** Amazon Prime counters this by offering a free permanent membership to **Grubhub+**, which waives delivery fees on eligible restaurant orders over $12.

---

## The Verdict: Which should you choose?

### Go with **Walmart+** if:
* **You want to save money upfront.** It is $41 cheaper per year than Amazon Prime.
* **You buy groceries regularly.** Same-day grocery delivery straight from your local Walmart is its strongest selling point.
* **You drive a lot.** The $0.10/gallon discount at major gas stations (including Sam's Club) adds up quickly.
* **You do a mix of online and in-store shopping.** Features like Scan & Go make in-person trips much faster.

### Go with **Amazon Prime** if:
* **You are a heavy online shopper.** You buy a wide variety of non-grocery items throughout the week and want them at your door in 1 to 2 days without thinking about order minimums.
* **You want a heavy entertainment bundle.** If you frequently use Prime Video, Prime Reading, and digital photo backup, the $139 fee easily justifies itself.
* **You use food delivery apps.** The free Grubhub+ subscription saves you a $10/month standalone fee.

By the way, to unlock the full functionality of all Apps, enable [Gemini Apps Activity](https://myactivity.google.com/product/gemini).

consensus.label: split
consensus.summary: ChatGPT favors Amazon Prime, Perplexity favors Walmart+, and Gemini refuses to pick a winner, reflecting genuine trade-offs between entertainment breadth versus practical cost savings.
